name: Environmental Storytelling
description: Narrative information delivered through placed objects, spaces, lighting, sound, bodies, props, or level composition rather than direct exposition.
design_notes: Environmental storytelling relies on authored clues and player interpretation. Define environmental_story_node_id, clue ids, discovery triggers, camera readability, optional interaction, localization for inspect text, and validation rules that keep clues visible after lighting, physics, or art changes.
design_purpose: Communicate backstory, mood, relationships, or consequences through the world itself.
Reward observation and exploration without forcing every story beat through dialogue.
Let level design, art, audio, and narrative collaborate on discoverable meaning.
player_fantasy: The player feels like an investigator piecing together what happened by reading the space.

parameters.name: environmental_story_node_id
parameters.description: Authored story node represented by placed world details.

parameters.name: authored_clue_id
parameters.description: Specific clue object or composition element in the scene.

parameters.name: discovery_trigger_id
parameters.description: Trigger that marks the clue discovered or records optional inspection.

parameters.name: world_state_flag_id
parameters.description: Flag changed or required by the environmental story node.

parameters.name: inspect_localized_string_id
parameters.description: Optional localization key for inspected clue text.

parameters.name: readability_probe_distance_units
parameters.description: Distance used to validate whether the clue can be seen or inspected.

edge_cases: Physics objects can move, break, or despawn before the player sees a required clue.
A lighting or camera pass can make the authored clue unreadable from normal player routes.
Quest state can change the meaning of a clue, requiring updated inspect text or spoiler gates.
Multiplayer players may discover the same clue at different moments and need shared or per-player journal state.
common_bugs: Discovery trigger fires through walls or floors because proximity checks ignore line of sight.
Clue placement works in editor but becomes hidden behind dynamic objects, weather, or NPC crowds.
Journal unlocks use scene object names instead of stable authored_clue_id records.
Localized inspect text references an outdated prop after art revisions.
balancing_notes: Optional environmental clues can be subtle; required clues need redundancy through layout, lighting, interaction prompts, or journal fallback.
Too many inspectable props reduce trust that any single clue matters.
Ambiguity is useful for mood, but quest-critical clues should have player-readable confirmation.
accessibility_notes: Offer inspect prompts, high-contrast clue highlighting, captions for ambient story audio, and journal summaries for discovered clues.
Do not rely only on tiny prop details, color, or spatial audio for required story comprehension.

implementation_notes.unity: Attach stable authored_clue_id data to story props or trigger volumes and validate readability with editor tooling. Discovery should commit through a narrative service that updates world_state_flag_id and optional journal entries idempotently.
implementation_notes.godot: Use Area nodes or interactables with exported environmental_story_node_id and localized inspect text. Save discovered ids through a narrative-state autoload instead of relying on scene node names.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use Actor Components, Data Assets, or Gameplay Tags for clue ids and discovery triggers. Editor validation can check line of sight, distance, and required quest links before a level ships.
implementation_notes.web: Represent environmental clues as data attached to scene objects or tile markers. On discovery, commit stable clue ids and world flags before showing inspect UI or adding journal summaries.

relationships.reason: Environmental clues can unlock lore records when the player examines or discovers authored story nodes.
Top-down views can make subtle environmental clues too visible or too small unless composition is tuned.
Environmental clues should avoid relying only on hue or low-contrast markings for interpretation.

example_games.title: BioShock
example_games.note: Uses spaces, props, and audio context to communicate world history.

example_games.title: Gone Home
example_games.note: Uses environmental details and discovered objects to build narrative understanding.
